superseded_note: Operational role taken over by APEX_DOCTRINE_v1.0.md (v1.0-PROVISIONAL) per APEX_PARALLEL_MERGE_PLAYBOOK.md step 5. This rules file retained as historical evidence; not deleted. Rule IDs in the locked doc are authoritative going forward.
amendment_note: R15 added; O8 and C1-C3 amended to point at compound closure signal. Provisional until SP-A.2 lock.

rules.text: Use both Claude and Codex; neither alone is acceptable.
Plan before code; 30 to 90 minutes planning per feature; zero code until plan survives Codex.
Iterate until effectively silent per R15: two consecutive adversarial passes with no fresh CRITICAL or HIGH findings, plus one clean ship-gate review.
Codex enriches Claude Code; it does not replace it.
Claude is trigger-happy at ship time; Codex review gate mandatory before ship.
Claude excels at copywriting, design thinking, and certain coding patterns.
Codex executes targeted numbered steps with ~9/10 reliability.
Codex is more token-efficient on targeted edits than Claude.
Codex is stronger on edge cases, security, privacy, scaling/perf, project hygiene, and second/third-order consequences.
Hand keyboard to Codex when Claude is weak in a domain.
Pure code review (no edit, not steerable) maps to /codex:review.
Adversarial review of a specific plan or diff maps to /codex:adversarial-review.
Holistic full-codebase rescue maps to /codex:rescue (async).
Continuous auto-review reserved for production-sensitive work.
Everyday code review pre-ship via /codex:review.
Adversarial planning loop until Codex silent; block forward motion until silent.
Background rescue in flow state; never block the queue.
Pre-ship Debbie Downer review focused on PII, security, privacy, perf, and 2nd/3rd-order effects.
Full feature cycle: plan -> adversarial loop -> implement -> background rescue -> ship gate.
Pass --background for async; pass explicit scope/path for adversarial review; record model.
Default model gpt-5-codex high reasoning; fallbacks gpt-4.1, gpt-4o; never silent-fall-back.
Async by default for /codex:rescue; audits run 4-25 minutes.
/codex:review is not steerable; use /codex:adversarial-review for any pinpoint.
New thread by default for adversarial review; existing thread only when continuity adds signal.
Persist every Codex run transcript to apex_governance/codex_runs/PHASE-N/<job-id>.txt.
Every revision is an artifact; no batch-skipping findings; severity is Codex's call.

Phase advances on R15 compound closure signal: two consecutive adversarial passes with no fresh CRITICAL or HIGH findings, plus one clean ship-gate review. Medium/low residuals are policy contributions, not blockers.
No automated ship action proceeds without a clean /codex:review on the final diff.
Claude may delegate implementation to Codex; record reason in the WO.
Default stack $100 Claude Max + $20 ChatGPT/Codex; free tier acceptable for light use.
Reserve PT4-style continuous auto-review for production-sensitive work only.
Route token-efficient targeted edits to Codex executor mode.
Forbidden: solo Claude runs that ship without Codex review.
Forbidden: skipping findings Claude labels minor.
Forbidden: closing the loop after one clean adversarial run.
Forbidden: falling back to Claude self-review when Codex plugin is unreachable.
Forbidden: trying to steer /codex:review.
Forbidden: running large rescues in foreground and blocking the queue.
Cert must show plan reached effective silence per R15 (two consecutive passes, no fresh CRITICAL/HIGH; medium/low logged as policy contributions).
Cert must show diff reached effective silence per R15.
Cert must show tests reached effective silence per R15.
Cert must show clean /codex:review ship gate.
Cert must log model and reasoning effort for every Codex call.
Cert must show --background used for reviews predicted >60s.
Cert must link archived transcripts under apex_governance/codex_runs/PHASE-N/.
Cert must cite doctrine rule IDs next to every conformance claim.

Closure-rule amendment. The compound closure signal is: (a) two consecutive adversarial-review passes with no fresh CRITICAL or HIGH findings — medium/low residuals are policy contributions logged to the absorbing SP, not blockers; AND (b) one clean ship-gate /codex:review pass. Adversarial review enumerates angles and is never the literal no-findings gate; ship-gate review is severity-filtered and IS the binding clean-gate. Provisional until SP-A.2 ratifies.

Loop caps. Per-artifact adversarial-review cap: 8 passes. HIGH-forever = 3 consecutive passes with fresh HIGH or CRITICAL findings. Per-phase total codex spend cap: 30 calls (configurable in phase_budget_SP-X.Y.json). Per-phase wall-clock cap: 4 hours of active loop time. Any cap firing pauses iteration and fires R17.

Arbiter pattern. When R16 caps fire, summon an independent context (spawn-context skill, optionally a parallel codex --background thread) that has not seen the loop. Provide artifact + transcripts + unresolved HIGH/CRITICAL + cap-fired. Arbiter outputs ONE of {structural reshape, doctrine amendment candidate, accept-as-is rationale} as JSON to findings/SP-X.Y.arbiter.json. The in-loop codex then adversarial-reviews the arbiter proposal. If proposal passes R15, apply. If not, fire R18. Single-shot per phase.

Rescue + split. When R17 fails: codex:rescue --background on the artifact tree, drop stuck-loop WO, split per L2 (convergent artifact + policy feeder). Only after R18 fails does the phase go BLOCKED awaiting human direction.

Mandatory pre-flight research. Before any spec, run INTERNAL research (scan local repos: C:/Users/Owner/Repos, Projects, X:/GitHub, X:/Projects, X:/Automations, X:/Bermech, X:/Workspace, X:/Claude, apex/registry muscles) and EXTERNAL research (GitHub/PyPI/npm/HuggingFace/awesome-lists). Outputs: internal-research.md with READY-TO-USE/EXTEND/REFERENCE-ONLY/OBSOLETE classification; external-research.md with ADOPT-AS-DEP/ADOPT-WITH-FORK/STUDY-FOR-PATTERN/REJECT decisions; research-decision.md with REUSE/EXTEND/GREENFIELD verdict. GREENFIELD requires enumerated rejected candidates. Decision is codex-reviewed before spec opens.

Stuck-loop re-research. When R16 caps fire, before R17 arbiter, automatically re-run internal+external research scoped to the artifact's stuck point. Arbiter receives the re-research output as additional context.
